title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After discussing the murder of Joyce Reynolds with Superintendent Spence, Hercule Poirot travels to "Apple Orchard," a Georgian-style red-brick house, to meet Mrs. Oliver. Poirot explains that he has chosen to stay in a hotel rather than at Miss Judith Butler's house to maintain a detached perspective for his observations. He outlines his investigative strategy, which includes examining the crime scene, interviewing Joyce's mother, and meeting with the local inspector, the local doctor, and the school headmaster. Poirot also plans to utilize Spence’s connections to the local police and intends to involve Spence's sister, Elspeth, who possesses significant knowledge of the local community. He schedules a meeting for six o'clock at the Spence residence to discuss the case with the siblings.

At Apple Orchard, Mrs. Oliver introduces Poirot to Mrs. Dreyfus, a tall, athletic, and efficient woman in her mid-forties who organized the social event where the murder occurred. Poirot observes that Mrs. Dreyfus appears to be suppressing her emotions, suspecting she feels anger or inadequacy because a tragedy took place during an event she arranged. Mrs. Dreyfus expresses her hope that Poirot can assist with the investigation, noting the local commotion caused by the child's death and commenting on the rising frequency of such crimes in the countryside.
